meta_title: For Freelancers: project management to Solve can't debug code (Beginner Idea List)
meta_description: Learn how to project management and overcome can't debug code. A Beginner-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: Project Management to Solve Debugging Woes
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with broken code. Learn how to use project management, Antigravity, and Claude Code to debug faster and impress your clients.
content.answer_block: Debugging isn't just about syntax; it's a project management failure. By structuring your codebase into modular, trackable components and leveraging AI-driven CLI tools like Claude Code, you can isolate errors in minutes, maintain client velocity, and deliver production-ready code without the typical architectural debt.
content.intro:
The screen flickers, a cryptic error message mocks your progress, and your client's deadline is breathing down your neck. You are trapped in the 'debugging death loop,' where every fix seems to spawn two new bugs. It is a freelancer's worst nightmare: the moment your professional reputation clashes with uncooperative code.

But the best builders don't just 'code harder.' They architect for failure. By shifting your mindset from reactive patching to proactive project management, you can turn a chaotic codebase into a predictable, high-velocity machine. Let's reclaim your workflow and ship with confidence.

steps.title: Architectural Audit
steps.description: Before fixing bugs, map your dependencies to understand the blast radius of your code.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze this codebase and generate a dependency graph. Identify top 3 high-risk modules prone to bugs.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'map the dependency graph of this directory and list high-risk files'

steps.title: Isolation Strategy
steps.description: Use project management principles to isolate the bug to a single module, preventing side effects.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a reproduction script for the current error, isolating the logic into a standalone test file.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'create a minimal reproduction test case for the auth module failure'

steps.title: Automated Instrumentation
steps.description: Inject logging that tracks state transitions, making hidden bugs visible.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Add structured telemetry to the identified module to track state changes during execution.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'instrument the data processing flow with structured logging'

steps.title: Systematic Patching
steps.description: Apply fixes through a structured PR process to ensure no regression.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor this function to handle edge cases identified in the logs. Ensure type safety.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'apply the patch and run existing unit tests to verify stability'

steps.title: Verification & Documentation
steps.description: Finalize the fix by updating documentation to prevent future occurrences.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Update the README and internal docs explaining the bug and the fix implemented.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate documentation for the recent fix in the project wiki'

pro_tips: Always treat a 'bug' as an opportunity to write a new unit test; if you can't test it, it isn't fixed.
Use Claude Code to generate 'post-mortem' reports for every major client bug to show you are proactively managing their product.
Never debug in production; always clone the environment to a temporary branch using your project management tool.
If a bug takes more than 60 minutes to solve, stop coding and perform a 'Rubber Duck' session with Antigravity to re-evaluate the architecture.
Maintain a 'Bug Register' in your project management software to identify patterns in your own coding style.
content.next_steps: Integrate your bug tracking into your client dashboard and start using Claude Code for your next feature sprint.
content.conversion_block: Stop wasting hours on manual debugging and start building like a pro. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
